def_en: An immoral act considered to be against divine law; transgression of God's will
def_zh: 罪；罪恶；违背神旨意的行为
quote_en: She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins. (—Matthew 1:21)
quote_zh: 她将要生一个儿子，你要给他起名叫耶稣，因他要将自己的百姓从罪恶里救出来。（—马太福音 1:21）
ex_en: The shepherd who follows Christ knows that no darkness can hide sin, for the light of righteousness exposes the wilderness within, calling the repentant heart to worship in truth.
ex_zh: 跟随基督的牧人知道，没有任何黑暗能隐藏罪，因为公义的光照亮内心的旷野，呼唤悔改的心用真理来敬拜。

def_en: To become pregnant; to form or devise an idea
quote_en: "The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel" (which means "God with us"). (—Matthew 1:23)
quote_zh: “必有童女怀孕生子；人要称他的名为以马内利。”（以马内利翻出来就是“神与我们同在”。）（—马太福音 1:23）
ex_en: The humble soul does not arrogate glory for what God conceives within; like Mary, she accepts the sacred call with meekness, trusting that righteousness will be fulfilled.
ex_zh: 谦卑的灵魂不将神所怀的工据为己有；像马利亚一样，她以温柔接受神圣的呼召，相信公义必得成就。

def_en: To guide, protect, and care for a group of people; to lead as a pastor
quote_en: "But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for out of you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel." (—Matthew 2:6)
quote_zh: “犹大地的伯利恒啊，你在犹大诸城中并不是最小的；因为将来有一位君王要从你那里出来，牧养我以色列民。”（—马太福音 2:6）
ex_en: Christ came to shepherd the lost, calling sinners to repent and follow, for the blessed peacemaker knows the Shepherd's voice and walks in humility.
ex_zh: 基督来牧养失丧的人，呼唤罪人悔改跟随，因为蒙福的使人和睦者认识牧人的声音，并在谦卑中行走。

def_en: To show reverence and adoration for God; to honor with religious rites
quote_en: On coming to the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense and myrrh. (—Matthew 2:11)
quote_zh: 进了房子，看见小孩子和他母亲马利亚，就俯伏拜那小孩子，揭开宝盒，拿黄金、乳香、没药为礼物献给他。（—马太福音 2:11）
ex_en: Those who worship in spirit and truth do not merely serve with empty rituals; their hearts hunger and thirst for righteousness, and their lives become a fragrant offering.
ex_zh: 用心灵和真理敬拜的人，不单以空洞的礼仪事奉；他们的心饥渴慕义，生命成为馨香的供物。

def_en: To turn away from sin and change one's mind, heart, and direction toward God
quote_en: and saying, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near." (—Matthew 3:2)
quote_zh: “天国近了，你们应当悔改！”（—马太福音 3:2）
ex_en: To repent is not merely to mourn one's sin, but to turn from the wilderness of self and follow the Shepherd, who alone can make the meek inherit the earth.
ex_zh: 悔改不只是为自己的罪哀恸，更是从自我的旷野回转，跟随那位唯一能让温柔的人承受地土的牧人。

def_en: A desolate, uninhabited area; a place of testing and spiritual preparation
quote_en: This is he who was spoken of through the prophet Isaiah: "A voice of one calling in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.'" (—Matthew 3:3)
quote_zh: 这人就是先知以赛亚所说的：“在旷野有人声喊着说：‘预备主的道，修直他的路。’”（—马太福音 3:3）
ex_en: Even in the wilderness, the pure in heart find God; for when one repents and prepares the way, the blessed light breaks through the desolation.
ex_zh: 即使在旷野，清心的人也能遇见神；因为当人悔改预备道路时，蒙福的光就冲破荒凉。

def_en: The act of turning from sin and changing one's heart and mind toward God
quote_en: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance. (—Matthew 3:8)
quote_zh: 你们要结出果子来，与悔改的心相称。（—马太福音 3:8）
ex_en: True repentance bears fruit that surpasses mere words; the merciful heart produces righteousness, and the humble soul glorifies the Father through deeds.
ex_zh: 真实的悔改结出超越言语的果子；怜悯的心生出公义，谦卑的灵魂借着行为荣耀天父。

def_en: To immerse in water as a sign of spiritual purification and initiation into the Christian faith
quote_en: "I baptize you with water for repentance. But after me comes one who is more powerful than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire." (—Matthew 3:11)
quote_zh: “我是用水给你们施洗，叫你们悔改；但那在我以后来的，能力比我更大，我就是给他提鞋也不配。他要用圣灵与火给你们施洗。”（—马太福音 3:11）
ex_en: John came to baptize with water calling sinners to repent, but Christ baptizes with fire, refining the pure in heart who hunger for righteousness.
ex_zh: 约翰来用水施洗，呼唤罪人悔改，但基督用火施洗，炼净那些饥渴慕义的清心之人。

def_en: Moving downward; coming down from a higher place
quote_en: As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him. (—Matthew 3:16)
quote_zh: 耶稣受了洗，随即从水里上来；天忽然为他开了，他就看见神的灵仿佛鸽子降下，落在他身上。（—马太福音 3:16）
ex_en: When the Spirit comes descending like a dove, the humble heart recognizes the voice from heaven: "This is my beloved Son," and learns to serve with joyful obedience.
ex_zh: 当圣灵如鸽子降下时，谦卑的心认出从天上来的声音：“这是我的爱子”，并学会以喜乐的顺服来事奉。

def_en: Feeling satisfaction, joy, or approval; delighted
quote_en: And a voice from heaven said, "This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased." (—Matthew 3:17)
quote_zh: 从天上有声音说：“这是我的爱子，我所喜悦的。”（—马太福音 3:17）
ex_en: The Father is well pleased not with empty worship, but with those who follow the Son, proclaim His truth, and let their light shine before others.
ex_zh: 父所喜悦的，不是空洞的敬拜，而是那些跟随圣子、宣讲真理、让光照在人前的人。

def_en: The spoken or written expression of divine revelation; the message of God
quote_en: Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.'" (—Matthew 4:4)
quote_zh: 耶稣却回答说：“经上记着说：‘人活着，不是单靠食物，乃是靠神口里所出的一切话。’”（—马太福音 4:4）
ex_en: Those who hunger for righteousness feast on every word from God; they are blessed not by bread alone, but by the truth that surpasses worldly wealth.
ex_zh: 饥渴慕义的人以神口里所出的每一句话为粮；他们蒙福不单靠食物，而是靠那超越世上财富的真理。

def_en: To perform duties or services for; to obey and give worship to God
quote_en: Jesus said to him, "Away from me, Satan! For it is written: 'Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.'" (—Matthew 4:10)
quote_zh: 耶稣说：“撒但，退去吧！因为经上记着说：‘当拜主你的神，单要事奉他。’”（—马太福音 4:10）
ex_en: To serve God only is to reject the wilderness of worldly idols; the pure in heart worship Him alone, and their righteous deeds glorify the Father.
ex_zh: 单要事奉神，就是拒绝世界偶像的旷野；清心的人单单敬拜祂，他们公义的行为荣耀天父。

def_en: To proclaim or declare the message of God; to teach publicly about the kingdom of heaven
quote_en: From that time on Jesus began to preach, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near." (—Matthew 4:17)
quote_zh: 从那时候，耶稣就传起道来，说：“天国近了，你们应当悔改！”（—马太福音 4:17）
ex_en: Those who preach the kingdom call sinners to repent and follow; they are the blessed peacemakers who proclaim light in the darkness.
ex_zh: 传讲天国的人呼唤罪人悔改跟随；他们是蒙福的使人和睦者，在黑暗中宣讲光明。

def_en: To go after or accompany as a disciple; to obey and imitate
quote_en: "Come, follow me," Jesus said, "and I will send you out to fish for people." (—Matthew 4:19)
quote_zh: 耶稣对他们说：“来跟从我，我要叫你们得人如得鱼一样。”（—马太福音 4:19）
ex_en: To follow Christ is to leave behind the old self and repent; the meek who follow Him will inherit the earth and serve as light to the nations.
ex_zh: 跟从基督就是舍弃旧我并悔改；跟随祂的温柔人必承受地土，并成为列国的光。

def_en: To announce officially and publicly; to declare with authority
quote_en: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people. (—Matthew 4:23)
quote_zh: 耶稣走遍加利利，在各会堂里教训人，传天国的福音，医治百姓各样的病症。（—马太福音 4:23）
ex_en: The faithful proclaim good news to the crowds, not seeking their own glory, but pointing all to the Shepherd who heals the broken and blesses the meek.
ex_zh: 忠心的人向人群宣讲好消息，不求自己的荣耀，只指引众人归向那位医治破碎者、赐福温柔人的牧人。

def_en: A large group of people gathered together
quote_en: Large crowds from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea and the region across the Jordan followed him. (—Matthew 4:25)
quote_zh: 当下，有许多人从加利利、低加波利、耶路撒冷、犹太、约旦河外来跟着他。（—马太福音 4:25）
ex_en: The crowd followed Jesus not merely for miracles, but because the pure in heart hunger for righteousness; they sought the Shepherd who alone could satisfy their souls.
ex_zh: 人群跟随耶稣不单为神迹，而是因为清心的人饥渴慕义；他们寻求那位唯独能使人灵魂得饱足的牧人。

def_en: Receiving God's favor and happiness; spiritually fortunate
quote_en: "Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven." (—Matthew 5:3)
quote_zh: “虚心的人有福了，因为天国是他们的。”（—马太福音 5:3）
ex_en: Blessed are those who mourn their sin, for they shall be comforted; blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth and serve the King.
ex_zh: 哀恸自己罪的人有福了，因为他们必得安慰；温柔的人有福了，因为他们必承受地土并事奉君王。

def_en: To feel or express deep sorrow, grief, or repentance
quote_en: "Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted." (—Matthew 5:4)
quote_zh: “哀恸的人有福了，因为他们必得安慰。”（—马太福音 5:4）
ex_en: Those who mourn for righteousness' sake are blessed; their tears water the seeds of peace, and in their humility, the kingdom of heaven draws near.
ex_zh: 为义哀恸的人有福了；他们的泪水浇灌和平的种子，在他们的谦卑中，天国临近了。

def_en: Gentle, humble, and submissive to God's will; possessing strength under control
quote_en: "Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth." (—Matthew 5:5)
quote_zh: “温柔的人有福了，因为他们必承受地土。”（—马太福音 5:5）
ex_en: The meek do not arrogate power, but follow the Shepherd in humility; they are the pure in heart who shall see God and inherit the earth.
ex_zh: 温柔的人不僭取权柄，而是谦卑地跟随牧人；他们是必得见神的清心之人，并承受地土。

def_en: The quality of being morally right and just according to God's standards
quote_en: "Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled." (—Matthew 5:6)
quote_zh: “饥渴慕义的人有福了，因为他们必得饱足。”（—马太福音 5:6）
ex_en: Those who hunger for righteousness shall be filled; they proclaim truth in love, serve the humble, and their light shines before all.
ex_zh: 饥渴慕义的人必得饱足；他们以爱心宣讲真理，服侍谦卑的人，他们的光照在众人面前。

def_en: Showing compassion and forgiveness toward others
quote_en: "Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy." (—Matthew 5:7)
quote_zh: “怜恤人的人有福了，因为他们必蒙怜恤。”（—马太福音 5:7）
ex_en: The merciful heart reflects the Shepherd's love; they forgive as they have been forgiven, and their meekness surpasses the pride of the world.
ex_zh: 怜悯的心反映牧人的爱；他们怎样蒙赦免，也怎样饶恕人，他们的温柔胜过世界的骄傲。

def_en: Clean, untainted; sincere in heart and undivided in devotion to God
quote_en: "Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God." (—Matthew 5:8)
quote_zh: “清心的人有福了，因为他们必得见神。”（—马太福音 5:8）
ex_en: The pure in heart do not serve two masters; they worship God alone, and in their righteousness, they behold the glory of the kingdom.
ex_zh: 清心的人不事奉两个主；他们单单敬拜神，在他们的公义中，得见天国的荣耀。

def_en: One who reconciles others and promotes peace
quote_en: "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God." (—Matthew 5:9)
quote_zh: “使人和睦的人有福了，因为他们必称为神的儿子。”（—马太福音 5:9）
ex_en: Peacemakers follow the Prince of Peace; they proclaim reconciliation, mourn over division, and their merciful deeds glorify the Father.
ex_zh: 使人和睦的人跟随和平之君；他们宣讲和好，为分裂哀恸，他们怜悯的行为荣耀天父。

def_en: Suffering hostility and ill-treatment because of one's faith or beliefs
quote_en: "Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven." (—Matthew 5:10)
quote_zh: “为义受逼迫的人有福了，因为天国是他们的。”（—马太福音 5:10）
ex_en: Those persecuted for righteousness are blessed; they stand firm like the meek who inherit the earth, and their reward surpasses all earthly treasure.
ex_zh: 为义受逼迫的人有福了；他们像承受地土的温柔人一样站立得稳，他们的赏赐超越一切地上的财宝。

def_en: The quality of being salty; preserving and seasoning influence
quote_en: "You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled underfoot." (—Matthew 5:13)
quote_zh: “你们是世上的盐；盐若失了味，怎能叫它再咸呢？以后无用，不过丢在外面，被人践踏了。”（—马太福音 5:13）
ex_en: Without saltiness, the disciple loses purpose; but those who preserve righteousness and proclaim truth become light in the darkness, blessed to serve.
ex_zh: 失了咸味，门徒就失去了功用；但那些持守公义、宣讲真理的人成为黑暗中的光，蒙福事奉。

def_en: That which illuminates; spiritual truth and good example
quote_en: "You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden." (—Matthew 5:14)
quote_zh: “你们是世上的光。城造在山上是不能隐藏的。”（—马太福音 5:14）
ex_en: The light of the righteous cannot be hidden; it shines through meekness and mercy, calling all who hunger to follow the Shepherd.
ex_zh: 义人的光不能隐藏；它借着温柔和怜悯照耀，呼唤一切饥渴的人跟随牧人。

def_en: To praise and honor; to give glory to God
quote_en: In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven. (—Matthew 5:16)
quote_zh: 你们的光也当这样照在人前，叫他们看见你们的好行为，便将荣耀归给你们在天上的父。（—马太福音 5:16）
ex_en: When the pure in heart serve humbly and proclaim truth, they glorify not themselves but the Father, whose righteousness surpasses all human merit.
ex_zh: 当清心的人谦卑服侍、宣讲真理时，他们不荣耀自己，而是荣耀父，祂的公义超越一切人的功德。

def_en: To exceed; to be greater or better than
quote_en: For I tell you that unless your righteousness surpasses that of the Pharisees and the teachers of the law, you will certainly not enter the kingdom of heaven. (—Matthew 5:20)
quote_zh: 我告诉你们，你们的义若不胜于文士和法利赛人的义，断不能进天国。（—马太福音 5:20）
ex_en: True righteousness surpasses outward obedience; it springs from a pure heart that mourns sin, hungers for God, and glorifies the Father in all things.
ex_zh: 真正的义超越外在的顺服；它源于为罪哀恸、渴慕神、凡事荣耀天父的清心。
